tactical_shape: Defended stoically and relied on set-pieces
Hung in the game with a compact, resilient approach
standout_players: Abdulelah al-Amri scored the opener and was quickest to react for the goal
Hassan al-Tambakti helped create the goal with a header and defended well
Mohammed al-Owais made several good saves, including from Viñas, Ugarte, de la Cruz and Valverde
home_read.momentum: They took a point off a strong Uruguay side’s momentum early on, but the draw leaves them needing to respond against Spain.

tactical_shape: Marcelo Bielsa changed shape tactically by moving Federico Valverde into a more central role at half-time
standout_players: Ronald Araújo had Uruguay’s early chance and later rescued the point with the equaliser
Federico Valverde improved after being moved centrally and was involved in late chances
Fernando Muslera was beaten on Saudi Arabia’s goal and will have been annoyed at not catching the ball cleanly
Federico Viñas was involved in the key attacking moments, including the cross for Araújo and the headed assist in the equaliser
fatigue_injuries: Darwin Núñez looked off the pace throughout and was removed at half-time
Uruguay’s preparations were disrupted by a delayed flight from Cancún to Fort Lauderdale
away_read.momentum: They recovered to earn a deserved draw and look set up to press on, despite an underwhelming first half and travel disruption.
